text:
Comment:
On the basis of its "independent" foreign policy and as evidenced by its abstention during the UN voting on the embargo, Indonesia has been most reluctant to support the embargo on strategic materials to Communist China. Although forced to support it by a majority decision of UN members, the Indonesian Government undoubtedly desired to reemphasize its neutral position by omitting rubber from the strategic list. Despite the Indonesian delegate's contention that the cabinet might fall on the embargo issue, a firm government stand could easily override parliamentary opposition
